LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MICHAEL MCSHANE, PAUL ISAKSON'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO MAKE A TORT CLAIM WITHIN THE REQUISITE SIX MONTH PERIOD. (GOVT. CODE SECTION 900, ET SEQ.) AND FAILED TO APPLY FOR LATE CLAIM RELIEF WITHIN THE REQUISITE ONE YEAR PERIOD (GOVT. CODE SECTION 911.4). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings;
Off Calendar Feb-11-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MICHAEL MCSHANE, PAUL ISAKSON'S Motion for Judgment On The Pleadings. Appearances: Jason Lundberg, of Ladva Law Firm, (415) 296-8844, appearing for Plaintiff, and opposing party; Mark Lipton, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, appearing for Defendants, and moving party, CCSF, Michael McShane, Paul Isakson. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted without leave to amend. Plaintiffs failed to make a tort claim within the requisite six month period. (Govt. Code section 900, et seq.) and failed to apply for late claim relief within the requisite one year period (Govt. Code section 911.4). Order signed in Open Court.(302/CWW/DC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797
